name: Uttamur Viraraghavacharya
dates_notes: Born at Uttamanallur (Uthamur), Madhurāntakam taluk, Tamil Nadu; died 1995. First recipient of the Government of India President's Award for Sanskrit scholarship (1960). Honoured with the title Abhinava Deśika for his Vedānta-Deśika commentaries. Some biographical sources give 1897–1983; the 1903–1995 range follows the centenary-trust publication records.

dates_evidence.description: Government of India President's Award for Sanskrit scholarship, 1960 (first-ever recipient).
Sri Uttamur Viraraghavachariar Centenary Trust publication records (1997 onward).

core_thesis: Uttamūr Vīrarāghavācārya, honoured as *Abhinava Deśika*, is the twentieth-century Vaḍakalai Viśiṣṭādvaita scholar whose corpus restates the school's positions across the technical front. His approximately 185 Sanskrit and Tamil works comprise *vyākhyāna*, *darpaṇa*-gloss, and *bhūṣaṇa*-defence of the inherited canon (cf. Sri Uttamur Viraraghavachariar Centenary Trust, *Bibliography of Uttamūr Vīrarāghavācārya's works*, 1997). The magnum opus *Paramārtha-Bhūṣaṇa* defends Vedānta-Deśika's positions against later Advaita and Tenkalai criticism (*Paramārtha-Bhūṣaṇa*, the topical *pūrvapakṣa-siddhānta* sections on *upāya*, *prapatti*, and the status of Śrī). The *Sarvārtha-Siddhi-Vyākhyā* glosses Deśika's auto-commentary on the *Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa*; the *Śrī-Bhāṣyārtha-Darpaṇa* glosses Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* (per-*adhikaraṇa* gloss, on Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* on BS 1.1.1ff.); the commentaries on the *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi*, on Yāmuna's *Siddhi-traya*, and on Nadādūr Ammāḷ's *Tattva-Sāra* extend the work backward through the Śrīvaiṣṇava lineage. Three distinctive emphases run throughout. First, the Navya-Nyāya logical apparatus is integrated into Viśiṣṭādvaita exegesis to a degree no earlier modern commentator attempted (his commentaries on Udayana's *Nyāya-Kusumāñjali*, esp. *stabaka* 5 on *Īśvarānumāna*, and on Kaṇāda's *Vaiśeṣika-Sūtra* 1.1.4 *padārtha*-enumeration exhibit the technical command involved). Second, the pre-Deśika sources are systematically recovered (cf. his editions of Yāmuna's *Siddhi-traya* with vyākhyā). Third, *upāya-vaibhava* — works rather than effortless surrender — is defended as the Vaḍakalai soteriological backbone against the Tenkalai *mārjāra-nyāya* alternative (*Paramārtha-Bhūṣaṇa*, *upāya-prakaraṇa*, defending *markaṭa-nyāya* against *mārjāra-nyāya*; cf. Vedānta-Deśika, *Rahasya-Traya-Sāra* ch. 22 *Sādhanānuṣṭhāna-adhikāra*).

engaged_works.title: Paramartha-Bhushana
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed across his mature scholarly period.

ascription_evidence.description: Modern academic consensus; Centenary Trust publication records.

engaged_works.summary: An extensive Sanskrit polemical *prakaraṇa* defending Vedānta-Deśika's Vaḍakalai positions against later Advaita criticism (especially the Madhusūdana-line and the modern Holenarsipur revisionism) and against the Tenkalai *prapatti*-doctrine (*Paramārtha-Bhūṣaṇa*, *upāya-prakaraṇa*, on the Tenkalai *mārjāra-nyāya*). The treatise is organised by topic — ontology, *pramāṇa*-theory, *upāya*, the status of Śrī, the relation between the Lord and the *jīva* — and runs each topic as a *pūrvapakṣa*-*siddhānta* dialectic. The doctrinal payoff is the explicit re-establishment of Deśika's three-*pramāṇa* epistemology (*Paramārtha-Bhūṣaṇa*, *pramāṇa-prakaraṇa*, with reference to Vedānta-Deśika, *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* 1, *pramāṇa-paricchedaḥ*), of *yathārtha-khyāti* (*P-B*, *khyāti-prakaraṇa*; cf. Deśika, *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* 1, *khyāti-vāda*), and of *apṛthak-siddhi* (*P-B*, *sambandha-prakaraṇa*; cf. Deśika, *Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa* II.7–9 *apṛthak-siddhi-sara*), against challenges raised across six centuries of post-Deśika debate. The work is the school's modern reference text for Vaḍakalai apologetics, and is the principal evidence supporting Vīrarāghavācārya's *Abhinava Deśika* honorific.

engaged_works.title: Shri-Bhashyartha-Darpana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Rāmānuja's Śrī-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit *ṭīkā* on Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya*. The work proceeds *adhikaraṇa* by *adhikaraṇa* and supplies the Vaḍakalai modern reading at each contested locus, often in dialogue with Sudarśana Sūri's earlier *Śruta-Prakāśikā* (*Śrī-Bhāṣyārtha-Darpaṇa*, on Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* on BS 1.1.1 *jijñāsādhikaraṇa* with cross-reference to *Śruta-Prakāśikā* ad loc.). Three exegetical fronts dominate. First, the *samanvaya-adhyāya* loci that have always been the school's contested ground with Advaita — *tat tvam asi* (Chānd. 6.8.7), *neha nānāsti kiñcana* (Bṛh. 4.4.19; Kaṭha 2.1.11), *sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma* (Chānd. 3.14.1) — are read by the *ghaṭaka-śruti* method that lets identity-predication carry the body-soul relation rather than flat numerical identity (*Śrī-Bhāṣyārtha-Darpaṇa* on Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* on BS 1.1.4 *tat tu samanvayāt*; on BS 1.4.22 *prakṛtiś ca pratijñā-dṛṣṭāntānuparodhāt*). Second, the *avirodha-adhyāya* refutations of rival schools are updated against modern Advaita reformulations, especially Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi* (*Śrī-Bhāṣyārtha-Darpaṇa* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* on BS 2.1.14, *tad-ananyatva-adhikaraṇa*; on BS 2.2.27ff., the *anumeyatva-adhikaraṇa*). Third, the *sādhanādhyāya*'s treatment of *bhakti-yoga* (on BS 3.2.24 *anyārthaś ca parāmarśaḥ*; 3.4.26ff.) is connected forward to Vedānta-Deśika's *Rahasya-Traya-Sāra* (chs. 21–22) on the Vaḍakalai side of the *upāya*-question.

engaged_works.title: Sarvartha-Siddhi-Vyakhya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Vedānta-Deśika's Sarvārtha-Siddhi (auto-commentary on Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa).
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit sub-commentary on Vedānta-Deśika's *Sarvārtha-Siddhi*, the auto-commentary on the *Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa*. The work is the school's modern reference for Vaḍakalai ontology. Each of the *sara*-chapters of the *Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa* — on *jaḍa-dravya* (*Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa* I, *Jaḍa-Dravya-Sara*), *jīva-dravya* (TMK II, *Jīva-Sara*), *Īśvara-dravya* (TMK III, *Īśvara-Sara*), *nitya-vibhūti* (TMK IV, *Nityā-Sara*), *dharma-bhūta-jñāna* (TMK V, *Buddhi-Sara*) — is glossed with the doctrinal disambiguations the modern reader requires. The treatment of *apṛthak-siddhi* is unusually detailed (*Sarvārtha-Siddhi-Vyākhyā* on TMK II.7–9) and engages later scholastic objections, especially from the Madhva-line on whether *apṛthak-siddhi* collapses into either *samavāya* or *saṃyoga* (*S-S-V* on TMK II.7–9, with reference to Vyāsatīrtha, *Tarka-Tāṇḍava* on the *sambandha*-question). The treatment of *dharma-bhūta-jñāna* (*S-S-V* on TMK V.1–14) — the *jīva*'s constitutive substance-awareness, distinguished from awareness *simpliciter* — is similarly extended to engage post-Deśika Naiyāyika challenges. The *vyākhyā* is the natural counterpart to Vīrarāghavācārya's *Śrī-Bhāṣyārtha-Darpaṇa* on the *sūtra*-bhāṣya side: together they cover the school's two systematic load-bearing texts. The work is part of the modern Vaḍakalai pāṭhaśālā curriculum and supplies the standard reference for category-theoretic disputes within and across the school's external polemical horizon.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Parishuddhi-Vyakhya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored commentary on Vedānta-Deśika's Nyāya-Pariśuddhi.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on Vedānta-Deśika's *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi*. The work supplies the modern Vaḍakalai treatment of the school's epistemology — the three-*pramāṇa* scheme (*Nyāya-Pariśuddhi-Vyākhyā* on Deśika, *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* 1, *pramāṇa-paricchedaḥ*), the reduction of *upamāna* and *arthāpatti* to *anumāna* (*N-P-V* on *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* 2, *anumāna-paricchedaḥ*), the rejection of *anupalabdhi*, the defense of *yathārtha-khyāti* against *anyathā-khyāti* and *anirvacanīya-khyāti* (*N-P-V* on *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* 1, *khyāti-vāda*), the doctrine of *svataḥ-prāmāṇya* of *śabda* (*N-P-V* on *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* 3, *śabda-paricchedaḥ*). Vīrarāghavācārya's distinctive contribution is the engagement with twentieth-century Navya-Nyāya scholarship on *vyāpti* and *parāmarśa* (*N-P-V* on *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* 2, *anumāna* sections, with cross-reference to Gaṅgeśa, *Tattva-Cintāmaṇi*, *Anumāna-Khaṇḍa*), which lets the Viśiṣṭādvaita epistemology argue on equal terms with the still-living Naiyāyika pāṭhaśālā tradition. The treatment of perceptual error is unusually extended: where Deśika fixes *yathārtha-khyāti* in compressed form, Vīrarāghavācārya works through the post-Deśika challenges and supplies the modern defenses. The work is the standard modern reference for the school's epistemology and is part of the Vaḍakalai pāṭhaśālā curriculum at the Madras and Tirupati centres.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Kusumanjali-Vyakhya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored commentary on Udayana's Nyāya-Kusumāñjali.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on Udayana's *Nyāya-Kusumāñjali*. The work belongs to Vīrarāghavācārya's broader project of making Navya-Nyāya technical resources available within the Viśiṣṭādvaita pāṭhaśālā curriculum. Udayana's five *stabaka*-s on the *Īśvara*-inference (*Nyāya-Kusumāñjali* stabakas 1–5) are commented on with explicit alignment to Viśiṣṭādvaita ontology, where appropriate, and with explicit demarcation where Udayana's Naiyāyika premises diverge from the school's (*Nyāya-Kusumāñjali-Vyākhyā* on stabaka 1.1–22, on the *adṛṣṭa*-question; on stabaka 5.1–11, on the *kāryānumāna* for *Īśvara*). The treatment of the *kāryānumāna* — the inference from a created world to a creator — is unusually detailed (*N-K-V* on *Nyāya-Kusumāñjali* stabaka 5.1–11) and is read as supplementing the school's own *śarīra-śarīrin* metaphysics rather than as competing with it. The commentary is one of the principal pieces of evidence for the technical Navya-Nyāya competence on which Vīrarāghavācārya's reputation rests, and is read together with the *Vaiśeṣika-Rasāyana* and the *Mīmāṃsā-Nyāya-Prakāśa-Vyākhyā* as the core of his cross-darśana competence.

engaged_works.title: Vaisheshika-Rasayana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored commentary on Kaṇāda's Vaiśeṣika-Sūtra.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on Kaṇāda's *Vaiśeṣika-Sūtra*. The work makes the Vaiśeṣika categorial scheme available to Viśiṣṭādvaita exegesis as a precise technical contrast-class. Where the *Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa* fixes the school's ontology by argued contrast with rival category-schemes (*TMK* I, *Jaḍa-Sara*), the *Vaiśeṣika-Rasāyana* supplies the rival scheme in its own internal articulation. The *padārtha* enumeration (*Vaiśeṣika-Rasāyana* on *Vaiśeṣika-Sūtra* 1.1.4 *dharma-viśeṣa-prasūtād dravya-guṇa-karma-sāmānya-viśeṣa-samavāyānāṃ*), the atomism (*V-R* on VS 4.1.1ff., *paramāṇu-vāda*), the doctrine of *samavāya* (*V-R* on VS 7.2.26ff., *samavāya-prakaraṇa*), and the analysis of perceptible quality (*V-R* on VS 2.1–2.2, *guṇa-prakaraṇa*) each receive Vaḍakalai disambiguation. The treatment of *samavāya* is doctrinally consequential: Viśiṣṭādvaita rejects *samavāya* as a sui generis category and reduces it within the *apṛthak-siddhi* relation, and Vīrarāghavācārya's commentary supplies the modern argument for that reduction at the level of technical detail at which the Naiyāyika tradition operates (*V-R* on VS 7.2.26, with cross-reference to Deśika, *Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa* II.7–9 *apṛthak-siddhi-sara*). The work is unusual in twentieth-century Viśiṣṭādvaita literature for treating the Vaiśeṣika sympathetically as a precisely-constructed alternative rather than dismissively.

engaged_works.title: Mimamsa-Nyaya-Prakasha-Vyakhya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored commentary on Āpadeva's Mīmāṃsā-Nyāya-Prakāśa.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on Āpadeva's standard Pūrva-Mīmāṃsā primer. The work supplies the Vaḍakalai modern handling of the *vidhi*-*niṣedha*-*arthavāda* analysis the school inherits from its Mīmāṃsā background (*Mīmāṃsā-Nyāya-Prakāśa-Vyākhyā* on Āpadeva, *Mīmāṃsā-Nyāya-Prakāśa* §§ 1–24, the *vidhi-bheda* discussion), and clarifies how Viśiṣṭādvaita differs from orthodox Pūrva-Mīmāṃsā on the relative authority of *karma-kāṇḍa* and *jñāna-kāṇḍa* (*M-N-P-V* on *MNP* §§ 200ff., the *kratvartha-puruṣārtha* distinction). The standard Bhāṭṭa positions on *svataḥ-prāmāṇya* (*M-N-P-V* on *MNP* §§ 25–34, the *prāmāṇya-vāda*), *codanā* as the autonomous *pramāṇa* for *dharma* (*M-N-P-V* on *MNP* §§ 1–10, the *dharma-lakṣaṇa*), and the analysis of sentence-meaning (*M-N-P-V* on *MNP* §§ 50–80, the *vākyārtha* discussion) are commented on with explicit indication of where Viśiṣṭādvaita extends, modifies, or rejects them. The commentary is part of the school's modern Sanskrit pedagogy at the *pāṭhaśālā* level and supplies the link between the school's Mīmāṃsā background and its Vedāntic exegesis. It is read together with the *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi-Vyākhyā* on the epistemology side and the *Vaiśeṣika-Rasāyana* on the ontology side.

engaged_works.title: Prabandha-Raksha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored Tamil-Manipravala commentary on the Nālāyira-Divya-Prabandham.
engaged_works.summary: A Tamil-Maṇipravāḷa commentary on the *Nālāyira-Divya-Prabandham* — the Āḻvār Tamil-poetic corpus that constitutes one half of the Śrīvaiṣṇava *ubhaya-Vedānta*. The work matters because the Vaḍakalai school must defend the equal scriptural status of the Tamil Prabandham alongside the Sanskrit Veda (cf. Vedānta-Deśika, *Dramiḍopaniṣad-Tātparya-Ratnāvalī* and *Dramiḍopaniṣad-Sāra*, on the *ubhaya-Vedānta* doctrine), and a substantial Sanskrit-trained commentator on the Prabandham is therefore part of the school's apologetic apparatus. Vīrarāghavācārya's commentary integrates the Tamil-poetic devotional material with the Sanskrit-Vedāntic doctrinal vocabulary established in his other works, and supplies the modern Vaḍakalai bridge between the two registers of the school's authoritative corpus. The treatment of the *Tiruvāymoḻi* of Nammāḻvār (*Tiruvāymoḻi* 1.1.1 *uyarvaṟa uyarnalam*; 6.10.10 *agalakillen* the *carama-pāśura*; 10.10.11 the *phala-pāśura*), in particular, is conducted with explicit doctrinal coordination to the *Śrī-Bhāṣyārtha-Darpaṇa* on the Sanskrit side, so that the same Vaḍakalai positions on *prapatti*, *bhakti-yoga*, and the status of Śrī are exhibited from both sides of the *ubhaya-Vedānta* scriptural base.
